name: Bakeneko
description: The Bakeneko is a type of Japanese yōkai, or supernatural creature. It is a cat that has transformed into a monstrous form. Bakeneko are often depicted as large cats, sometimes standing on their hind legs, with the ability to shapeshift into humans, speak human languages, and even control the dead. They are said to gain these powers after living for many years (typically 13 years or more), reaching a certain size, or being allowed to lick lamp oil. Bakeneko are associated with misfortune, hauntings, and strange occurrences. They can cause fires, create illusions, and even curse their owners. While some tales portray them as malevolent, others depict them as mischievous but ultimately harmless. The Bakeneko serves as a cautionary tale about treating animals with respect, and the potential consequences of neglecting or mistreating them. They are a prominent figure in Japanese folklore and continue to appear in modern media.
subtitle: The shapeshifting monster cat of Japan
appearance: Bakeneko typically appear as ordinary domestic cats, but are often larger in size. They may walk on their hind legs, wear clothing, or carry towels. Some are depicted with a split tail, or a tail that is unusually long. Their eyes are often described as glowing or having an unnatural intensity. The color of their fur can vary, but black cats are frequently associated with Bakeneko transformations.
habitat: Bakeneko are typically found in human households, temples, or other places where cats commonly reside. They are often associated with older buildings or areas with a long history.
behavior: Bakeneko are known for their mischievous and sometimes malevolent behavior. They may cause disturbances, play pranks, or even attack humans. Some are said to drain the life force of their owners or cause them to fall ill. However, not all Bakeneko are evil; some are simply curious or playful, using their abilities to explore the world around them.

keyMyths.title: The Dancing Bakeneko
keyMyths.description: A Bakeneko was said to have been seen dancing in a graveyard, controlling the corpses of the dead. This myth highlights the Bakeneko's association with the supernatural and their ability to manipulate the spiritual realm.

keyMyths.title: The Lamp Oil Licker
keyMyths.description: The belief that a cat licking lamp oil could transform into a Bakeneko led to the practice of using paper lanterns instead of oil lamps. This demonstrates the cultural impact of the Bakeneko myth and its influence on daily life.

sources.author: Lafcadio Hearn
sources.work: Kwaidan: Stories and Studies of Strange Things
sources.description: A collection of Japanese ghost stories and folklore, including tales of supernatural cats.
sources.text: A collection of Japanese ghost stories and folklore, including tales of supernatural cats.

sources.author: Shigeru Mizuki
sources.description: A popular manga and anime series featuring various yōkai, including the Bakeneko.
